routePolicy: Public routes are presentation/publishing state, NOT identity: identity is canonicalId and the cross-edition pairing/order key is segmentPrefix; neither is ever a route. displayTitle (and its derived displaySlug) is the current editorial label and stays improvable forever -- URL permanence comes from a separate publishing layer, never from freezing the title. publicSlug is frozen at first publication (per edition) and stored in the per-work published-routes.yaml manifest. routePath uses the frozen publicSlug when present (urlStability 'stable'); otherwise the provisional displaySlug (urlStability 'provisional' if publishable, else 'draft'), which may change without a redirect until it is frozen. A deliberate publicSlug change is recorded in the manifest and 301-redirected; canonicalId never changes. Only 'stable' routes may be indexed/canonicalized. See docs/public-url-convention.md.
